seoTitle: Kurraba Point vs Kurrajong Heights — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kurraba Point and Kurrajong Heights. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Kurraba Point vs Kurrajong Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kurraba Point and Kurrajong Heights.
heading: Kurraba Point vs Kurrajong Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kurraba Point, New South Wales and Kurrajong Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kurraba Point has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Kurrajong Heights (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kurraba Point or Kurrajong Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kurraba Point has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Kurrajong Heights scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kurraba Point scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. Kurrajong Heights scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kurraba Point or Kurrajong Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kurraba Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
